title: Month of the Military Child Family Event at ERNC
location: Eagle River Nature Center, 32750 Eagle River Rd, Eagle River
description: Eagle River Nature Center hosts a family afternoon in partnership with Girl Scout Troop 21010 from JBER, including activities for all ages and a bear and moose safety talk at 1 PM.

title: Everyday Anchorage: Duke Russell & Ward Wells — Exhibition Opening
description: New exhibition opening May 1 capturing daily life in Anchorage through paintings and photography from two local artists, Duke Russell and Ward Wells. On view through winter 2027.

title: Michaela Goade: Song of the Seasons — Exhibition Opening
description: New exhibition opening May 1 celebrating Alaska's seasons through images, sketches, and process videos by Tlingit and Norwegian artist and author Michaela Goade. Features her creative process behind beloved picture books.

title: AK Grown: Childhood in Alaska — Exhibition Opening
description: Photographs spanning from the 1930s to the present trace nearly a century of childhood across Alaska, exploring how the state's youngest residents experience their world.

title: 39th Annual Kenai Peninsula Sport, Rec & Trade Show
location: Soldotna Regional Sports Complex (Soldotna Field House), Soldotna
description: The largest retail trade show on the Kenai Peninsula, featuring over 100 vendors, fishing trip bookings, archery, a youth fish pond, mechanical bull, and food. Opening night Friday includes food and beer with entry.
sourceName: Kenai Peninsula Sport Rec & Trade Show

title: Yonder Mountain String Band
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Progressive jamgrass band Yonder Mountain String Band performs in support of their 11th studio album 'Nowhere Next,' blending bluegrass, indie rock, and country. Presented by the Anchorage Concert Association.

title: Anchorage Salmon Run 5K/10K
description: The 35th annual Anchorage Salmon Run, a community road race featuring 5K and 10K distances that supports students through the PTA. Open to runners and walkers of all levels.

title: Pink Cheeks Triathlon
location: Seward High School, 2100 Swetman St, Seward
description: Seward Parks & Recreation's annual reverse triathlon (5K run, 10K bike, 600-yard pool swim) open to all ability levels as solo competitors or 3-person relay teams. Course includes cross-country trails, city streets, and inclines.

title: Steps for Strong Minds 5K Run/Walk
description: A family-focused 5K run and walk event raising awareness and funds for mental health. Open to all ages and abilities.

title: KPHA Spring Meeting at Pratt Museum
description: Kenai Peninsula Historical Association spring meeting with a 10 AM session, potluck lunch, and afternoon program of local historical interest. Open to the public.

title: Love a Nurse Run 5K
description: A 5K run and walk benefiting the Foundation for Alaska Nurses, celebrating nursing professionals. Open to all fitness levels.

title: Girls on the Run 5K
description: Girls on the Run season-ending 5K celebratory race promoting confidence and healthy habits in girls through running. Open to participant girls and community supporters.

title: Jo Koy — Stand-Up Comedy
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Filipino-American comedian Jo Koy performs stand-up at the Alaska Airlines Center, a large-capacity arena show.

title: Get Moving Mat-Su Valley Dash 5K
description: Community 5K road race in Wasilla encouraging active living in the Mat-Su Valley.

title: Anchorage Bike to Work Day
description: Annual citywide celebration encouraging Anchorage residents to commute by bicycle, organized as part of Bike Month. Pit stops with refreshments are set up along major routes throughout the day.

title: Arctic Marine Science: Sikuliaq to Shore — Exhibition Opening
description: New exhibition opening May 15 following the research vessel Sikuliaq into Arctic science in action, connecting maritime research to shore-based understanding of the Arctic environment.

title: Seward Mermaid Festival
description: Annual two-day festival coinciding with Seward Harbor Opening Weekend, featuring vendors, live music on the main stage, a mermaid costume contest, and a Saturday parade. Created by local businesses to celebrate the start of summer season.

title: Chiodos at Williwaw Social Outdoors
location: Williwaw Social Outdoors, 609 F Street, Anchorage
description: Post-hardcore band Chiodos performs at Williwaw's outdoor venue in downtown Anchorage.

title: Anchorage Market & Festival — Opening Weekend
description: Opening weekend of the season-long Anchorage Market & Festival, featuring 300+ vendors across 7 acres with Alaskan arts, crafts, handmade jewelry, and food vendors offering reindeer sausage, salmon dishes, Hmong specialties, and more. Free admission.

title: Seward Salmon & Song Festival
description: Second annual outdoor music festival at Miller's Landing featuring nationally and locally renowned live music, art and food vendors, and camping. Rooted in environmental conservation with a focus on Resurrection Bay chinook salmon.

title: Gold Nugget Triathlon
location: Bartlett High School, 1101 Golden Bear Dr, Anchorage
description: All-women sprint triathlon dedicated to improving the lives of women and girls through athletics. Includes in-person and virtual divisions, open to ages 10 and up.

title: Shoreline Mafia
description: West Coast hip-hop group Shoreline Mafia performs at Williwaw in downtown Anchorage.

title: Alaska Arbor Day Tree Planting Volunteer Event
description: Annual Alaska Arbor Day volunteer event inviting community members to plant trees in Anchorage parks, coordinated by the Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: SOJA
location: Moose's Tooth Pub & Pizzeria, 3300 Old Seward Hwy, Anchorage
description: Reggae rock band SOJA performs live at Moose's Tooth Pub & Pizzeria in Anchorage.

title: Gunsight Mountain Music Fest 2026
location: Gunsight Mountain Lodge, 123 N. Glenn Hwy, Chickaloon
description: Three-day outdoor music festival headlined by Confederate Railroad in the Mat-Su Valley foothills. Weekend camping event with multiple acts in a remote mountain lodge setting.

title: Trent Waldron Half Marathon & 10K
location: Margaret Eagan Sullivan Park, 1824 W 15th Ave, Anchorage
description: Anchorage Running Club-hosted half marathon and 10K road race starting and finishing at Margaret Eagan Sullivan Park. Course cut-off is noon. Virtual option runs May 14–31.

title: Anchorage Market & Festival — Weekend 2
description: Second weekend of the Anchorage Market & Festival's 2026 season, featuring 300+ vendors with Alaskan crafts, art, and diverse food options. Saturdays 10 AM–6 PM, Sundays 10 AM–5 PM. Free admission.

title: 3 Rings Circus
description: Family circus event running two days at Sullivan Arena with multiple performances. All-ages attraction featuring classic circus acts.

title: Sawyer Brown & Montgomery Gentry
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Country acts Sawyer Brown and Montgomery Gentry perform together at the Alaska Airlines Center arena in Anchorage.

title: Purity Ring
location: Wendy Williamson Auditorium, 2533 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Canadian electronic music duo Purity Ring performs at the Wendy Williamson Auditorium on the UAA campus in Anchorage.

title: Eagle River Nature Center — Little Explorers Guided Hike
location: Eagle River Nature Center, 32750 Eagle River Rd, Eagle River
description: Toddler-paced guided hike along the Rodak Trail for babies and children up to age 6 accompanied by an adult. No registration required; check in by 11 AM at the main Nature Center building.

title: Chase Matthew
location: George M. Sullivan Sports Arena, 1600 Gambell St, Anchorage
description: Country artist Chase Matthew performs live at the Sullivan Sports Arena in Anchorage.

title: Twilight 12K & Skinny Mini 6K
location: Start: 8th & H St (Skinny Raven Downtown); Finish: Rose Garden, Delaney Park Strip, Anchorage
description: Evening road race closing the A/C Street bridge for a scenic route through Government Hill, downtown Anchorage, the Coastal Trail, and Westchester Lagoon — with live bands along the course. Post-race celebration at the Rose Garden features Bear Tooth food and Broken Tooth beverages.

title: Anchorage Market & Festival — Weekend 3
description: Third weekend of the Anchorage Market & Festival's 2026 season with over 300 Alaskan vendors, local food, handmade crafts, and live entertainment. Free admission.

title: Anchorage Tacos and Tequila Fest
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell St, Anchorage
description: Outdoor food and drink festival focused on tacos and tequila, held in the Sullivan Arena parking lot in Anchorage.
